title: triclisia coriacea
intro: Triclisia coriacea, also known as the 'African Dogbane', is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its root, which contains alkaloids that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Triclisia coriacea include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, as well as its use in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Triclisia coriacea typically involve drying and grinding the root, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or powdered extracts for medicinal use.
